title: Scourge (Victory I-class)

The '''''Scourge''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]] that was active and well known in the [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. Manufactured by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]], the ''Scourge'' was 900 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length. Commanded by [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Alpiett Gredge]], the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] patrolled the area around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]], and regularly inspected the [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] two military [[Space station/Legends|space stations]], [[Station 2LC/Blue]] and [[Station 1B6/Green]].

==Description==
The ''Scourge'' was a [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]], 900 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, and manufactured by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]]. It possessed a Class 1 primary [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], with a Class 15 as backup in case the other malfunctioned. Additionally, the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] had functional [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], and [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]. A full crew of 4,798 personnel and 402 [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] would operate the ''Scourge'', though the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] could run on a skeleton crew of 1,785 and fifteen gunners if need be. It also had enough room to transport 2,040 [[Soldier/Legends|troopers]]. The ''Scourge'' held enough consumables to last its crew four [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and had a cargo capacity of 8,100 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]]. Armaments-wise, the [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]] possessed ten [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]], forty double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]], eighty [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launchers, and ten [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors. The ''Scourge'' had a maximum speed of 800 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] in a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]].<ref name="Orbit" />

==History==
[[File:Alpiett.png|thumb|left|200px|Vice Admiral Alpiett Gredge, the commander of the ''Scourge'']]
The ''Scourge'' was one of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy's]] Star Destroyers that was well known in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]]<ref name="Orbit" /> as of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{FarOrbitDate}}</ref> when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was engaging the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Commanded by [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Alpiett Gredge]], the ''Scourge'' patrolled the restricted area surrounding the planet [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]], and also regularly inspected the two Imperial border outposts in the sector: [[Station 2LC/Blue]], a [[Bavos-I heavy-duty military space platform]], and [[Station 1B6/Green]], a [[Bavos-II heavy-duty military space platform]]. The ship also frequently stopped at 2LC/Blue to supply the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] with deliveries varying from new crew members to cargo pods filled with supplies. When Gredge learned that some of the crew of the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'' had [[Mutiny on the Far Orbit|mutinied]] from under Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Vocis Kenit]], the ''Scourge''{{'s}} [[Commander/Legends|commander]] was happy to provide any help he could to Kenit in tracking the rogue vessel.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Scourge'' was helmed by Vice [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Alpiett Gredge, a respected and honorable commander who was an unwavering supporter of the Imperial [[Navy/Legends|Navy]].<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Scourge'' was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]]. The book was a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and was a sequel of sorts to the previous publication, ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', carrying on that book's theme by following the ''Far Orbit''{{'s}} adventures.

The ''Scourge'' is one of the key players in the scenario hook "Who is the More Foolish…" and the adventure idea "Smash the ''Scourge''!" The former involves the ''Scourge'' being drawn away from Ralltiir by the ''Far Orbit''{{'s}} raids on 1B6/Green, leaving the Rebels on Ralltiir with more room to breathe. "Smash the ''Scourge''!" involves the Star Destroyer being lured to an ambush point by the ''Far Orbit'' where an Alliance taskforce captures or destroyes Gredge's vessel. However, neither of these adventures are [[canon]], as they conflict with the [[Capture of Coh Veshiv|events]] of the adventure scenario ''[[The Capture of Coh Veshiv]]'', which is considered the better choice as it is a full blown scenario. The conflict between ''The Capture of Coh Veshiv'' and the scenarios in which the ''Scourge'' is featured arises because both lead to the Imperials fully going after the ''Far Orbit'' for the first time in the Darpa and [[Bormea sector/Legends|Bormea sectors]].
